q: What does Accenture call the layer of the metaverse that adds a sense of space to the digital world?
sa_answer:
Based on the documents provided, Accenture calls the layer of the metaverse that adds a sense of space to the digital world the "Internet of Place".

According to the report "Accenture-Metaverse-Evolution-Before-Revolution," the 3D evolution of the internet is what Accenture calls the "Internet of Place, adding a sense of space to the digital world and bringing digital elements to our physical lives." This layer is described as a continuum of virtual realms that includes virtual worlds where people can converse and trade, as well as an augmented reality (AR) layer that overlays our physical world.

The report also distinguishes this from the "Internet of Ownership," which is the infrastructure that allows people to carry their identity, money, and objects from place to place in the digital world, largely driven by the Web3 movement. Accenture states that while the Internet of Place and the Internet of Ownership can exist separately, "only together can they help to realize the full value of the metaverse."
